items.title: Former Titans scout Taylor found guilty of murder
items.summary: Blaise Taylor, a former defensive back at Arkansas State and NFL scout for the Titans, was found guilty of murdering his girlfriend and her unborn child Wednesday in a Nashville courtroom.

items.title: Chris Johnson aims to revive Ice Bucket Challenge
items.summary: Former NFL running back Chris Johnson is calling for the revival of the Ice Bucket Challenge to raise awareness of ALS as he fights the progressive nervous system disease.

items.title: Sorsby won't sue NFL, will prepare for '27 draft
items.summary: The NFL Players Association and Brendan Sorsby will not be pursuing litigation after the NFL's decision not to hold a supplemental draft this year, according to a memo sent to all 32 teams.

Without a first round pick, the Colts’ biggest moves this offseason were the re-signing […]

items.title: Fireworks, fireflies, and Colts football
items.summary: It’s summertime in Indiana. The days are hot, too hot with the current heatwave sweeping the nation, but news regarding the Indianapolis Colts is ice cold. The time between minicamps and training camp makes for some long days for those thirsting for football related updates. If there is a good time to take a break […]

items.title: Colts What If: What if Andrew Luck never retired?
items.summary: This six-part Colts What If series looks back at some of the biggest turning points in franchise history, from the Peyton Manning draft decision to playoff heartbreak, quarterback pivots and coaching chaos, while revisiting what happened, what could have changed, and how different the Colts might look if one major moment had gone the other […]

items.title: Colts Reacts Survey
items.summary: Welcome to SB Nation Reacts, a survey of fans across the NFL. Throughout the year we ask questions of the most plugged-in Colts fans and fans across the country. Sign up here to participate in the weekly emailed surveys.

There appears to be plenty left in the tank, and it could produce second thoughts for the Colts if they […]

items.title: Your guide to the upcoming 2026 NFL FLAG Championships in Westfield
items.summary: The world’s best youth flag football players are coming to central Indiana. From July 23–26, the 2026 NFL FLAG Championships presented by Toyota will take over Grand Park Sports Campus in Westfield — home of the Indianapolis Colts Training Camp —bringing together elite teams from across the country and around the globe.

Anarumo’s secondary had been boosted eight months earlier when [...]

items.title: Blue named NFL 'Mascot of the Year,' earning back-to-back honors for the Colts
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — For the second year in a row, the best mascot in professional football resides in the Circle City. The NFL announced its Mascot of the Year award on Tuesday, and the winner was none other than Blue. The face of the Indianapolis Colts, Blue has now won the award five times. This is [...]

items.summary: Created in 2006, this is Blue's fifth time winning the award and second year in a row taking it home.

One of the NFL’s premier running backs is entering the final year of his contract for a second time in four seasons, and his goal is simple. “Oh, I’d definitely love to be a Colt for life,’’ Taylor [...]

items.title: Return for Colts’ Alec Pierce likely to be mid-to-late training camp
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Alec Pierce’s return from ankle surgery remains a moving target. The Indianapolis Colts’ veteran receiver, who signed a four-year, $114 million contract in March that made him one of the NFL’s highest-paid players at his position, has been a spectator during the team’s offseason workouts. That includes the ongoing mandatory minicamp. Pierce underwent [...]

items.summary: Pierce underwent a procedure this spring after a PRP injection didn't alleviate an ankle issue he'd been dealing with throughout 2025.
